title: identity — developer configuration
description: Subset of the identity-tier config relevant to the `identity` module itself. Auth-specific keys (session TTL, JWT signing, cross-app SSO grant TTL) live in `identity/auth/config/auth.php`. MFA-specific keys (TOTP period, WebAuthn attestation) live in `identity/mfa/config/mfa.php`. See design.md §6.

password.description: Password policy — enforced by the `strong_password` validation rule at every write. Tenant admins can tighten via settings but the compliance floor is the config default.

keys.description: Minimum character count. NIST 800-63B recommends ≥ 8; we default to 12 for defence-in-depth.
Off by default — NIST 800-63B explicitly advises against mandatory symbols on the theory they push users toward predictable patterns. Tenants that need it for a compliance regime can flip it on.
Check every password against haveibeenpwned's k-anonymity API before accepting it. Off = no external call.
Reject passwords seen in >=N public breaches. Default 1 = reject anything ever seen. Loosen (e.g. 100) for tenant environments with older users who resist strong-password requirements.
Rolling window — password can't match any of the last N hashes. Enforced by the `not_in_password_history` rule.
argon2id preferred. bcrypt fallback for environments without libsodium. Configured on the Hash facade at boot.
Argon2id memory cost in KiB. Default = 64 MiB. Higher = more resistant to GPU cracking; lower = faster hash.
Argon2id iteration count.
Argon2id parallelism factor. Keep at 1 on cloud runtimes with shared CPU.
Force rotation after N days. Null = never force. NIST 800-63B advises against mandatory rotation — default null. Set to 90/180/365 only when a compliance regime requires.

lockout.description: Failed-login lockout policy. Progressive backoff punishes repeated attackers without permanently locking out legitimate users.

keys.description: Number of failures before locking. Counter resets on successful login.
Sliding window over which failed_attempts_count accumulates.
Initial lockout duration.
Double the lockout duration on each subsequent lockout within a 1-hour window. 15min → 30min → 60min → 120min (capped).
Cap on lockout duration. Default 12 hours.
Whether new identities MUST verify email before their Users become active. False = accept unverified identities (rare — usually only for admin-provisioned identities).
Rate-limit on the resend endpoint.
Per-email rate limit on the forgot-password endpoint. Rejected requests still return 200 (enumeration safety).

mfa_secret.description: KMS envelope-encryption settings for Identity.mfa_secret_encrypted. Configuring here — not in the mfa module — because the encryption is a property of the Identity storage substrate.

keys.description: Which encryption backend the MfaSecretEncryptor uses. 'kms' = AWS KMS (production); 'vault' = HashiCorp Vault; 'local' = local key (dev-only).
KMS key ARN or alias. E.g. `alias/stackra/identity-mfa`.
Dev-only. Never used in production.

How long a soft-deleted Identity is retained before hard delete. GDPR-compliant erasure requires prompt action (30 days is a common industry standard); a compliance officer can extend via settings up to 90 days for pending disputes.
When true, every mutating Artisan command must be invoked with --reason=<text> (audit-logged). Off in dev; on in staging + prod.
